methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Xaira Therapeutics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Seed), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Trilobio is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Roya Amini-Naieni and Maximilian Schommer that has raised $11M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 3 public peers in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Xaira Therapeutics, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics.

Of three valuation methods tested, only stage-progression triangulation proved applicable. This method applies a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from seed to next stage, using the $11M total raised as a proxy for current value. This yields an implied valuation of $28M. Peer-median analysis was not executable due to insufficient narrow-stage peers in the biotech universe (only 2 comparable datapoints available, below the 3-company threshold). Public-comp anchoring using the sector P/S median of 6.1x could not be applied, as private companies rarely disclose revenue figures.

The single applicable method supports a valuation range of $23M to $32M, with a central estimate of $28M. This range reflects inherent uncertainty in seed-stage valuation: the absence of peer multiples limits confidence in the stage-progression multiplier itself, and lack of financial disclosure prevents cross-validation through market multiples. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.5x step-up assumption for this specific company's risk profile and trajectory.
